fact: October 26 is exactly 299 days into the year, which means there are only 66 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun is setting almost 2 minutes earlier each day around this time in most places, making the days shrink super fast!
October 26 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season - the zodiac sign represented by a creature that glows under black light!
Maple trees are dumping their leaves like crazy right now in northern climates, and a single large maple can drop over 600,000 leaves in one autumn!
Why do spiders seem to be everywhere in late October? Because this is when house spiders are desperately looking for warm places to spend winter!
Around this date, the North Pole is getting only about 2 hours of sunlight per day as winter approaches!
Monarch butterflies are flying over 2,000 miles south right now, and the ones alive today have never made this journey before - they just know how to do it!
October 26 is roughly 35 days after the autumn equinox, which means we're officially deep into the 'getting darker fast' part of the year in the north!
Bears in cold regions are eating up to 20,000 calories per day around this time - that's like eating 40 hamburgers every single day!
Morning frost typically starts showing up regularly around this date in many northern places, creating those cool ice crystals that look like tiny glass needles on grass!
Geese flying south right now can travel over 1,500 miles without stopping, and they honk constantly to keep their flock together during these epic journeys!
